uses: Castilleja minor, also known as the pink lady's slipper orchid, has been traditionally used in folk medicine. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers are believed to have medicinal properties. parts: Castilleja minor, also known as dwarf Indian paintbrush, is a perennial plant native to North America. intro: Castilleja minor, also known as Indian paintbrush, is a perennial wildflower native to the northwestern region of North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing relief from conditions such as bronchitis, coughs, and sore throats. The bioactive constituents of Castilleja minor include alkaloids, including ajugalin and gentianine, as well as glycosides and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Castilleja minor is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are traditionally used to treat respiratory issues and as a natural remedy for colds and flu.
